title: [Bug]: "relabel: invalid mount option" when used in Dockerfile RUN
body:
### Issue Description

The `relabel=shared` mount option works as expected when used in `podman run`, but does not work under `podman build` when used in Dockerfile's RUN entry.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

1. Create Dockerfile:

```
FROM docker.io/ubuntu:22.04
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=dir,target=/test,relabel=shared cat /test/file.txt
#RUN --mount=type=bind,source=dir,target=/test cat /test/file.txt
```

2. Create `test` directory and put something into `dir/file.txt`:

```
$ mkdir -p dir
$ echo hello >dir/file.txt
```

3. Run `podman build -t defanator:1 .`.

### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman build -t defanator:1 .
STEP 1/2: FROM docker.io/ubuntu:22.04
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=bind,source=dir,target=/test,relabel=shared cat /test/file.txt
Error: building at STEP "RUN --mount=type=bind,source=dir,target=/test,relabel=shared cat /test/file.txt": resolving mountpoints for container "eab51fc761f38ee47e777badb2c4668cc42e0dd7ad2b363c822cba0c5dff260e": relabel: invalid mount option
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
$ podman build -t defanator:1 .
STEP 1/2: FROM docker.io/ubuntu:22.04
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=bind,source=dir,target=/test cat /test/file.txt
hello
COMMIT defanator:1
--> afbaa2945ae
Successfully tagged localhost/defanator:1
afbaa2945ae52e996a31883703f38153b875d7b4040c70638a225a32e72fab0d
```

Without `relabel=shared` option build works just fine (result depends on an initial labeling of the `dir` directory).

When running command directly, everything works just fine:

```
$ podman run -ti --mount type=bind,src=dir,target=/test,relabel=shared ubuntu:22.04 cat /test/file.txt
hello
```

### Upstream Latest Release

eases the transition from other container engines and allows the management of
pods, containers and images.  Simply put: alias docker=podman.
Most podman commands can be run as a regular user, without requiring
additional privileges.

podman uses Buildah(1) internally to create container images.
Both tools share image (not container) storage, hence each can use or
manipulate images (but not containers) created by the other.

100% reproducible.

comments.body:
Hi @defanator , There is no `relabel` key in buildah you can directly use 

If ( selinux ) is enabled.
```
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=dir,target=/test,shared,z cat /test/file.txt
```
or

If ( selinux) is disabled.
```
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=dir,target=/test,shared cat /test/file.txt
```

I hope this helps.
Thanks @flouthoc, this worked perfectly. It wasn't very clear though that mount flags/options do have different syntax in podman-run vs podman-build.
We should support the same syntax.
Buildah should gain support for relabel=shared

~~Buildkit names this parameter as `sharing` instead of `relabel` so should we do that.~~

Edit: My bad this is exclusive to mount=type=cache we can introduce relabel to bind mount
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I can take a look at this cc/ @flouthoc
@danishprakash Sure take a look if you want and you can always assign back to me if you want.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan, there's an active PR with the changes, I'm waiting on some inputs from @flouthoc on the tests, whenever he's available. Should not be open for long.
Yes I remember seeing the PR after I wrote the comment. Thanks for your work.
